title: Document the security command
goal: Write docs/interactive/security.md and docs/non-interactive/security.md mirroring the adversarial-project docs (range rules, no-flags = last completed phase, the .mini/security/<range>.md report output, and the inline-not-scoped caveat), and add a Security review row to the Review table in docs/README.md.

steps.title: CLI page docs/non-interactive/security.md
steps.detail: Write the reference page mirroring non-interactive/adversarial-project.md: synopsis (no-flags + phase/ref forms), range table, no-flags = last completed phase (preSha->HEAD, genesis fallback), report written to .mini/security/<range>.md with the naming scheme (phase-<id>, range-<A>-<B>, range-<short>-<short>), scoped tool set (read + read-only git + Write(.mini/security/**), no Edit, no findings write). Verifiable: file exists, links resolve.

steps.title: Slash page docs/interactive/security.md
steps.detail: Write the page mirroring interactive/adversarial-project.md: what it does, In-a-session steps passing $ARGUMENTS to mini context security, an example. Explicitly add the caveat that an inline run does NOT enforce the scoped tool set (unlike the terminal mini security) -> prefer a terminal run or /clear. Verifiable: file exists, links to the CLI variant and back resolve.

steps.title: Security review row in docs/README.md
steps.detail: Add a 'Security review of a phase or range' row linking interactive/security.md and non-interactive/security.md to the Review-section table, next to the adversarial rows. Verifiable: the row is in the table and both links point to the newly created files.

steps.title: Accuracy + link verification
steps.detail: Check both pages against the real behavior (phase-mode needs the next phase's preSha; inline != scoped) and confirm every relative link points to an existing file (grep targets). Verifiable: no link points to a missing file, caveats match the code.

resolvedVerify: Read the two new docs pages for tone/clarity
